clinical_notes: Requires 36 hour washout if switching from ARNI to ACEi. Start lisinopril at 2.5 to 5mg. Monitor potassium and creatinine in 1 to 2 weeks.
Can switch directly from ARNI to ARB without washout. Start at equivalent valsartan dose. Monitor potassium and creatinine in 1 to 2 weeks.
Direct substitution within SGLT2i class. Same monitoring requirements.
Different beta blocker profiles. Carvedilol has alpha blocking activity. When switching, start carvedilol at 3.125mg BID regardless of metoprolol dose. Titrate independently.
When switching from carvedilol, start metoprolol succinate at 12.5 to 25mg daily. Titrate independently.
Spironolactone is lower cost alternative. Same potassium monitoring. May cause gynecomastia unlike eplerenone.
Eplerenone has fewer anti androgenic side effects. Higher cost. Same potassium monitoring.
Torsemide has better bioavailability and longer duration. Convert dose approximately 1:4 (torsemide:furosemide).
Bumetanide has more predictable absorption. Convert approximately 40mg furosemide to 1mg bumetanide.
Direct ACE inhibitor substitution. Enalapril dosed twice daily unlike lisinopril once daily.
